packageRules.description: Python interpreter bumps (requires-python) and the matching python-base container image ship together in one PR, separate from regular 3pp bumps. Manual review: bumping the interpreter is rarely a no-op for our 3pp set, so we don't want this automerged alongside boto3 patches.
Pair the python-base Docker image (builder + runner, any registry path) with the interpreter bump above via the shared groupSlug.

Keep pyproject.toml and the lockfile in sync by raising the declared floor when a new in-range version is released, and updating the lockfile in the same PR. Example: `cachetools >=5,<8` locked at 7.1.2 becomes `>=7.1.4,<8` with uv.lock at 7.1.4 — humans reading pyproject.toml see the actual minimum tested version, and downstream consumers resolving from our published wheels get the same floor. Renovate runs the manager's lock tool (`uv lock`, `poetry lock`, etc.) after the manifest edit, so bump covers what update-lockfile would have done. Scoped to lockfile-aware managers only: pip_requirements and pip_setup are intentionally excluded because they typically have no separate lockfile.
